tools.name: search_papers
tools.description: Search arXiv papers by query. Supports field prefixes: ti (title), au (author), abs (abstract), cat (category), all (everything). Combine with AND, OR, ANDNOT.

query.description: Search query. Examples: "transformer attention", "au:vaswani AND ti:attention", "cat:cs.CL AND ti:language model"

tools.name: get_paper
tools.description: Get details for a specific arXiv paper by ID. Returns title, authors, abstract, categories, PDF link, and journal reference.

arxiv_id.description: arXiv paper ID, e.g. "2301.07041" or "2301.07041v2" for a specific version

findings.title: Token Exposure — server version disclosure in HTTP response headers
findings.description: HTTP response header 'server: cloudflare' discloses internal information.
findings.remediation: Remove the 'server' response header in production.

findings.title: 'search_papers' accepts missing required fields
findings.description: Tool succeeded with empty arguments despite required: ['query']
findings.remediation: Validate that all required parameters are present before processing.

findings.title: 'search_papers.query' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent NoneType value 'None' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'search_papers' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'search_papers' returns oversized response
findings.description: Tool 'search_papers' returned 12,479 bytes (threshold: 8,192 bytes). Large responses can overflow LLM context windows, push out system prompts, or cause denial-of-service in agent pipelines.
findings.evidence:
12,479 bytes — preview: Found 225018 results (showing 1–10)

**A simple way to speedup Gauss Elimination**
ID: 1101.4081v3 | math.NA
Authors: Sivaram Ambikasaran
Published: 2011-01-21

This article looks at a simple modifica
findings.remediation: Paginate or cap tool responses. Never return unbounded data. Aim for responses under 4 KB to preserve LLM context budget.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: initialize crash — missing protocolVersion causes unhandled error
findings.description: Sending initialize without the required protocolVersion field caused the server to crash or return an unexpected result. Servers must validate required fields and return -32602 (Invalid params).
findings.remediation: Add required-field validation to the initialize handler.

findings.title: initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)
findings.description: The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.
findings.remediation: Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 2 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Server accepts requests without jsonrpc field
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request missing the required 'jsonrpc' field. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, this field is mandatory and must equal '2.0'.
findings.remediation: Validate that the 'jsonrpc' field is present and equals '2.0' before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for non-conformant messages.

findings.title: Server accepts array request ID — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request with an array as the request ID. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires the id to be a string, number, or null.
findings.remediation: Validate that the request 'id' is a string, number, or null. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for array or object IDs.
